整套首页
unit_18 0% 正确率 0 复习 0 错题

unit_18

推荐练习默认不提示本单元词。需要复盘时可手动显示本单元词；提交后判分，也可放弃查看答案。

50 推荐题 329 全部题目 0 弱相关题 30% 推荐答案命中比例
#1 Sentence Equivalence
Individuals interested in longevity have sought to fine-tune their bodies with all kinds of ______ diets: only raw foods, only plants, only the flesh, fruit, and nuts that prehistoric humans would have hunted and foraged.Individuals interested in longevity have sought to fine-tune their bodies with all kinds of ______ diets: only raw foods, only plants, only the flesh, fruit, and nuts that prehistoric humans would have hunted and foraged.
答案：D, F　proscriptive; exacting
finefine-tunelongevity
fine: standard unit headword | fine-tune: standard unit headword | longevity: standard unit headword
#2 TC TRIPLE
Native American art is not a (i) __________ enterprise: it reflects differences among artists' personal, community, and tribal experience. As Native American artists continue to create the art that is valid for them, however, the (ii) __________ in Native American art between traditional and modern styles (primarily promulgated by non-Native American critics) has become outmoded. There is heightened recognition that placing Native American art in these two opposing categories has had a (iii) __________ effect on both artists and viewers.Native American art is not a (i) __________ enterprise: it reflects differences among artists' personal, community, and tribal experience. As Native American artists continue to create the art that is valid for them, however, the (ii) __________ in Native American art between traditional and modern styles (primarily promulgated by non-Native American critics) has become outmoded. There is heightened recognition that placing Native American art in these two opposing categories has had a (iii) __________ effect on both artists and viewers.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, H　monolithic; supposed distinction; deleterious
supposesupposeddeleterious
suppose: standard unit headword | supposed: standard unit headword | deleterious: standard unit headword
#3 Text Completion
The science of astronomy was begun by amateurs and today remains dependent on their contributions, which are incisive by virtue of being ______ by the a priori assumptions that often vitiate the work of professional research scientists.The science of astronomy was begun by amateurs and today remains dependent on their contributions, which are incisive by virtue of being ______ by the a priori assumptions that often vitiate the work of professional research scientists.
答案：B　unencumbered
a priorivirtuevitiate
a priori: standard unit headword | virtue: standard unit headword | vitiate: standard unit headword
#4 Text Completion
Substantial atmosphere around planetary bodies acts as (i)______ incoming objects. Smaller objects, particularly those that are lower in density and more fragile, (ii)______ in the upper reaches of the atmosphere, whereas more intact, larger bodies may survive to impact the surface. Thus, relative to large craters, small craters are much less (iii)______ on bodies with dense atmosphere, such as Earth, Venus, and Titan, than they are on Mercury and Moon.Substantial atmosphere around planetary bodies acts as (i)______ incoming objects. Smaller objects, particularly those that are lower in density and more fragile, (ii)______ in the upper reaches of the atmosphere, whereas more intact, larger bodies may survive to impact the surface. Thus, relative to large craters, small craters are much less (iii)______ on bodies with dense atmosphere, such as Earth, Venus, and Titan, than they are on Mercury and Moon.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, I　significant filters to; vaporize; common
denseagglomeratecrater
dense: standard unit headword | agglomerate: standard unit headword | crater: standard unit headword
#5 TC DOUBLE
Some dietary experts suggest that we should (i) _________ the traditional notion that low-fat diets are salutary, since, according to these researchers, a low-fat diet has (ii) __________ body weight or general health, and focus instead on the quality of the carbohydrates we are eating.Some dietary experts suggest that we should (i) _________ the traditional notion that low-fat diets are salutary, since, according to these researchers, a low-fat diet has (ii) __________ body weight or general health, and focus instead on the quality of the carbohydrates we are eating.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, D　jettison; no special benefit for
notionespousedeleterious
notion: standard unit headword | espouse: standard unit headword | deleterious: standard unit headword
#6 Sentence Equivalence
The beauty of the scientific approach is that even when individual researchers do ______ bias or partiality, others can correct them using a framework of evidence on which everyone broadly agrees.The beauty of the scientific approach is that even when individual researchers do ______ bias or partiality, others can correct them using a framework of evidence on which everyone broadly agrees.
答案：C, E　succumb to; yield to
shrinkoverreactsuccumb

答案：A　an inscrutable

#8 TC TRIPLE
Since readily available computer programs now allow photographers to manipulate their images with the same (i) __________ as painters, photography's claim to any inherent truth has just about evaporated. But the (ii) __________ possibilities of photography have been present from the medium's birth. Distortions of truth not only occur mechanically (through shutter speed, for example) and in printing (as through choice of chemicals and paper) but are (iii) __________ to the very idea of photographers being stylistically different from one another.Since readily available computer programs now allow photographers to manipulate their images with the same (i) __________ as painters, photography's claim to any inherent truth has just about evaporated. But the (ii) __________ possibilities of photography have been present from the medium's birth. Distortions of truth not only occur mechanically (through shutter speed, for example) and in printing (as through choice of chemicals and paper) but are (iii) __________ to the very idea of photographers being stylistically different from one another.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, E, H　license for fiction; fictive; integral
methodicalextraneous
methodical: standard unit headword | extraneous: standard unit headword
#9 SE
Whereas ground-based interferometers detect astrophysical events briefly and infrequently, space-based ones should hear a __________ of signals as soon as they turn on, including a constant chorus from binary white dwarfs in our galaxy.Whereas ground-based interferometers detect astrophysical events briefly and infrequently, space-based ones should hear a __________ of signals as soon as they turn on, including a constant chorus from binary white dwarfs in our galaxy.
答案：B, C　plethora; profusion
plethoraprofusion

Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, E　not uncommon; exotic
landscapearticulate

#11 Text Completion
Given children’s active fantasy lives, one might think of truthfulness as a young child’s (i)______ virtue. It turns out that lying is the more (ii)______ skill. A child who is going to lie must recognize the truth, intellectually conceive of an alternate reality, and be able to convincingly sell that new reality to someone else. Therefore, lying (iii)______ both cognitive development and social skills that honesty simply doesn’t require.Given children’s active fantasy lives, one might think of truthfulness as a young child’s (i)______ virtue. It turns out that lying is the more (ii)______ skill. A child who is going to lie must recognize the truth, intellectually conceive of an alternate reality, and be able to convincingly sell that new reality to someone else. Therefore, lying (iii)______ both cognitive development and social skills that honesty simply doesn’t require.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, I　an acquired; advanced; demands
fantasyalternatevirtue
fantasy: standard unit headword | alternate: standard unit headword | virtue: standard unit headword
#12 Sentence Equivalence
In Ramachandran’s opinion, it is perfectly acceptable to propose bold speculations about the brain, even if these speculations seem ______; as Ramachandran frequently remarks, science thrives on risky conjecture.In Ramachandran’s opinion, it is perfectly acceptable to propose bold speculations about the brain, even if these speculations seem ______; as Ramachandran frequently remarks, science thrives on risky conjecture.
答案：A, F　unfounded; baseless
prematureunfounded
premature: standard unit headword | unfounded: standard unit headword
#13 Text Completion
Many Latin American writers and critics have come to bristle at the very mention of the type of fiction termed “magic realism,” but to the common reader the appeal of such fiction is ______.Many Latin American writers and critics have come to bristle at the very mention of the type of fiction termed “magic realism,” but to the common reader the appeal of such fiction is ______.
答案：B　undiminished
termappeal
term: standard unit headword | appeal: standard unit headword
#14 Sentence Equivalence
Excessive focus on what might have been can cause in us feelings of restlessness and regret, but some scientists are beginning to think that fancying an alternative reality might have ______ effects as well.Excessive focus on what might have been can cause in us feelings of restlessness and regret, but some scientists are beginning to think that fancying an alternative reality might have ______ effects as well.
答案：C, F　restorative; tonic
alternativeadverse
alternative: standard unit headword | adverse: standard unit headword

The maps in this volume are meant not as guides but as ______: they are designed to make the reader think anew about the city.The maps in this volume are meant not as guides but as ______: they are designed to make the reader think anew about the city.
答案：D　provocations
volumeadornment
volume: standard unit headword | adornment: standard unit headword

The monitoring is not ______; on the contrary, the defendant and his or her attorney are required to be given notice of the government’s listening activities.The monitoring is not ______; on the contrary, the defendant and his or her attorney are required to be given notice of the government’s listening activities.
答案：C　surreptitious
circumspectsurreptitious

答案：A, E　upheaval; convulsion
affluencelurch

The only (iii)______ sensory deprivation of the sessions came from the handsome color slides favored by the corporate presenters.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, E, I　opulence; ascetic; respite from

forswear
forswear: standard unit headword
#20 TC SINGLE
According to the ancient Chinese philosopher Mozi, the world's problems stem from ____________: rather than desiring what is best for the community, people care most about themselves, then their family and friends, and little about others.According to the ancient Chinese philosopher Mozi, the world's problems stem from ____________: rather than desiring what is best for the community, people care most about themselves, then their family and friends, and little about others.
答案：E　partiality
stem
stem: standard unit headword
#21 TC TRIPLE
The only way to definitively test a large civil engineering structure such as a bridge is to build it in anticipation of possible challenges from nature and then let nature take its course. That is not to say that every new large structure is a (i) __________. Engineers understand a great deal about the behavior and limitations of their structures' components. Furthermore, it is the (ii) __________ structure that is built more than 10 or 20 percent larger than its predecessors, thus allowing engineers to move (iii) __________ into unknown territory.The only way to definitively test a large civil engineering structure such as a bridge is to build it in anticipation of possible challenges from nature and then let nature take its course. That is not to say that every new large structure is a (i) __________. Engineers understand a great deal about the behavior and limitations of their structures' components. Furthermore, it is the (ii) __________ structure that is built more than 10 or 20 percent larger than its predecessors, thus allowing engineers to move (iii) __________ into unknown territory.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, E, G　gamble; unusual; incrementally
predecessor
predecessor: standard unit headword

答案：C, E　ephemeral; transitory

答案：B　minute
prominent

For example, one ought not dedicate to a worthy recipient an object that one feels is devoid of value.Some norms governing authors' dedications of their works are (i) __________ the norms governing ordinary speech (it may be wrong to lie, and thus wrong to lie in a dedication), but other norms are (ii) __________ dedications. For example, one ought not dedicate to a worthy recipient an object that one feels is devoid of value.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, F　indistinct from; particular to

答案：E, F　convoluted; intricate
meander

Archaeopteryx had feathers on its hind limbs that helped stabilize it and acted as air brakes, allowing the otherwise __________ creature to turn more adeptly and fly steadily.Archaeopteryx had feathers on its hind limbs that helped stabilize it and acted as air brakes, allowing the otherwise __________ creature to turn more adeptly and fly steadily.
答案：B　ungainly
ungainly

#27 TC TRIPLE
When the introduction of a nonnative species into a habitat correlates with the decline of a species native to that habitat, observers may (i) __________ a causal relationship. However, there is little empirical evidence for a relationship between invasion and extinction, and it is not (ii) __________ for native and nonnative species to thrive after the nonnative species' introduction, because the habitat's conditions favor both. When native species do decline after nonnative species are introduced, the reason may be a human-caused habitat alteration that affects native and nonnative species (iii) __________.When the introduction of a nonnative species into a habitat correlates with the decline of a species native to that habitat, observers may (i) __________ a causal relationship. However, there is little empirical evidence for a relationship between invasion and extinction, and it is not (ii) __________ for native and nonnative species to thrive after the nonnative species' introduction, because the habitat's conditions favor both. When native species do decline after nonnative species are introduced, the reason may be a human-caused habitat alteration that affects native and nonnative species (iii) __________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, E, H　infer; unusual; differentially
alteration
alteration: standard unit headword

Blank 3

The fishing industry has (i)_______ populations of jellyfish predators, such as red tuna, swordfish, and sea turtles. And when small, plankton-eating fish, such as anchovies, are (ii)_______, jellyfish flourish, gorging on plankton and reproducing prolifically.It is hard to know exactly what is causing a worldwide proliferation of jellyfish, but we can speculate. The fishing industry has (i)_______ populations of jellyfish predators, such as red tuna, swordfish, and sea turtles. And when small, plankton-eating fish, such as anchovies, are (ii)_______, jellyfish flourish, gorging on plankton and reproducing prolifically.

Blank 2
答案：B, F　depleted; overharvested

Blank 3

Blank 2
Blank 3

Although a moment that excites our emotions is more likely to be recorded than a (iii)______ experience, the sensory qualities of an event also play a part in how vividly and accurately we remember it.The nature of our recollections is (i)______. One memory can seem (ii)______ whereas another must be coaxed out of our brain little by little. Although a moment that excites our emotions is more likely to be recorded than a (iii)______ experience, the sensory qualities of an event also play a part in how vividly and accurately we remember it.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, E, I　inconsistent; immediate and colorful; routine
coax

答案：A, E　hamstring; impair

答案：E　meretricious
illustrious

答案：B　declining

loquacious: standard unit headword
#53 Text Completion
For a time in the early Middle Ages, Latin culture came close to (i)______: the witness to that is the (ii)______ of manuscript copies of texts datable to the period. The process of copying manuscripts, the only way in which the fragile products of centuries of accumulating knowledge could be preserved, (iii)______, a situation that did not change for two and a half centuries, until the time of Charlemagne. In the intervening period, much of Classical literature was lost to us forever.For a time in the early Middle Ages, Latin culture came close to (i)______: the witness to that is the (ii)______ of manuscript copies of texts datable to the period. The process of copying manuscripts, the only way in which the fragile products of centuries of accumulating knowledge could be preserved, (iii)______, a situation that did not change for two and a half centuries, until the time of Charlemagne. In the intervening period, much of Classical literature was lost to us forever.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, I　vanishing; paucity; virtually came to a standstill
manuscript
manuscript: standard unit headword
#54 Text Completion
To the avid reader of E. O Wilson, much of his most recent book Consilience: The Unity of Knowledge will be (i)______, as the book represents the culmination of a life spent thinking about everything from the social lives of ants to the social lives of people. Nonetheless, new thoughts have been mixed in with the old to produce a book remarkable for its (ii)______ and ambition.To the avid reader of E. O Wilson, much of his most recent book Consilience: The Unity of Knowledge will be (i)______, as the book represents the culmination of a life spent thinking about everything from the social lives of ants to the social lives of people. Nonetheless, new thoughts have been mixed in with the old to produce a book remarkable for its (ii)______ and ambition.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, D　predictable; purview
culmination
culmination: standard unit headword
#55 Sentence Equivalence
In matters of taste, the art patron and collector Peggy Guggenheim was ______: she was for the strangest, the most surprising, the most satisfying, the best, the unique.In matters of taste, the art patron and collector Peggy Guggenheim was ______: she was for the strangest, the most surprising, the most satisfying, the best, the unique.
答案：D, F　an extremist; a zealot
neophyte
neophyte: standard unit headword
#56 Text Completion
Many scholars have argued that the United States Supreme Court usually (i)______ public opinion in its decisions because it fears that it will (ii)______ if it does not; when it does depart from public opinion, it whips up political maelstrom.Many scholars have argued that the United States Supreme Court usually (i)______ public opinion in its decisions because it fears that it will (ii)______ if it does not; when it does depart from public opinion, it whips up political maelstrom.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, D　hews to; lose public support
hew
hew: standard unit headword
#57 Sentence Equivalence
Experiments show that it is shockingly easy to elicit a sense of ______ among a group of strangers: just tell them they’ll be working as a team, and they immediately start working as a team.Experiments show that it is shockingly easy to elicit a sense of ______ among a group of strangers: just tell them they’ll be working as a team, and they immediately start working as a team.
答案：D, E　solidarity; camaraderie
obedience
obedience: standard unit headword
#58 Text Completion
The artist is known for making photographs that deals with political matters, yet because her art is open-ended and evocative, it would be wrong to characterize it as ______.The artist is known for making photographs that deals with political matters, yet because her art is open-ended and evocative, it would be wrong to characterize it as ______.
答案：A　polemical
open-ended
open-ended: standard unit headword
#59 Text Completion
It is only recently that emotion has attracted any substantial scholar attention, with historians lagging behind anthropologists, sociologists, and philosophers in their willingness to (i)______ emotion as subject worthy of scholarly attention. In the past, scholars viewed emotion as a natural and essential force that (ii)______ analysis—a strictly private matte—and therefore (iii)______ social life and the stuff of research.It is only recently that emotion has attracted any substantial scholar attention, with historians lagging behind anthropologists, sociologists, and philosophers in their willingness to (i)______ emotion as subject worthy of scholarly attention. In the past, scholars viewed emotion as a natural and essential force that (ii)______ analysis—a strictly private matte—and therefore (iii)______ social life and the stuff of research.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, E, H　recognize; defied; extraneous to

lag: standard unit headword | extraneous: standard unit headword
#60 Sentence Equivalence
Though Edmund certainly had a dignified bearing and made a great first impression, those who became acquainted with him soon realized he had an essentially ______ nature.Though Edmund certainly had a dignified bearing and made a great first impression, those who became acquainted with him soon realized he had an essentially ______ nature.
答案：A, F　pugnacious; truculent
complacent
complacent: standard unit headword

Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, D　admirable; unusual
assault
assault: standard unit headword

Section 4 of5|Question 2of 15 Mark Review Help Back Next Fillall blanks in the way that best completes the text. One school of thought regards job avoidance—missing work or arriving late—as (i)_____ leaving an unsatisfactory job.According to this view,job avoidance helps unhappy employees adjust to job frustrations and makes them less likely to quit. A competing school of thought suggests that job avoidance may simply (i)________propensities to leave.Section 4 of5|Question 2of 15 Mark Review Help Back Next Fillall blanks in the way that best completes the text. Blank 2
答案：A, E　an alternative to; reinforce

#63 Text Completion
Campus-wide discussion on academic integrity can be (i)______ by the fact that faculty and students tend to define cheating in (ii)______ ways. Even when they concur on what cheating means, faculty and students often assign different levels of severity to specific violations. These differences can serve as a major (iii)______ the creation of a commonly accepted set of standards of integrity that are consistently applied to all academic work within the campus community.Campus-wide discussion on academic integrity can be (i)______ by the fact that faculty and students tend to define cheating in (ii)______ ways. Even when they concur on what cheating means, faculty and students often assign different levels of severity to specific violations. These differences can serve as a major (iii)______ the creation of a commonly accepted set of standards of integrity that are consistently applied to all academic work within the campus community.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, H　hampered; disparate; impediment to
concur
concur: standard unit headword
#64 Text Completion
Given the many thematic strands that the book seeks to draw together into a continuous cord of narrative, it is perforce a work of ______, which is not to say that the author’s research fails to provide sufficient detail about each of his chosen themes.Given the many thematic strands that the book seeks to draw together into a continuous cord of narrative, it is perforce a work of ______, which is not to say that the author’s research fails to provide sufficient detail about each of his chosen themes.
答案：A　synthesis

thematic: standard unit headword

Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, E, G　rote work; extrapolation; inferred

#66 TC TRIPLE
Although often criticized by former allies for having abandoned or betrayed their common cause, Nguyen was actually remarkably (i) over the years. Examination of her career shows that she never ceased to use the best data she could find to illuminate the issues. If the data undermined positions she had previously (ii) she followed the data. If the data confirmed her earlier views, she clearly stated the fact. If the data were (iii) , she counseled caution.Although often criticized by former allies for having abandoned or betrayed their common cause, Nguyen was actually remarkably (i) over the years. Examination of her career shows that she never ceased to use the best data she could find to illuminate the issues. If the data undermined positions she had previously (ii) she followed the data. If the data confirmed her earlier views, she clearly stated the fact. If the data were (iii) , she counseled caution.

Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, D, H　consistent; espoused; insufficient

espouse: standard unit headword

Paintings created in India during the Mughal dynasty were ______ in ambition but ornamental in presentation: in one direction they have an affinity with newspaper photographs, while in the other they have the intricacy of jewels.Paintings created in India during the Mughal dynasty were ______ in ambition but ornamental in presentation: in one direction they have an affinity with newspaper photographs, while in the other they have the intricacy of jewels.
答案：B　documentary

Blank 1
Blank 2

Celebration was, however, premature: subsequent research has established that the observed phenomena were likely caused by (iii) ___________ declines in industrial and natural emissions.In 1998, observations of a (i) __________ in the growth of atmospheric methane, a potent greenhouse gas, suggested that many of the scenarios for global warming provided in a 1995 assessment may have been overly (ii) __________. Celebration was, however, premature: subsequent research has established that the observed phenomena were likely caused by (iii) ___________ declines in industrial and natural emissions.

Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, I　slowdown; gloomy; temporary

#72 SE
The scientific study of laughter faces a frustrating paradox: ideal experiments take place in rigidly controlled environments with all but the most salient variables eliminated, but such sterile settings are often __________ laughter.The scientific study of laughter faces a frustrating paradox: ideal experiments take place in rigidly controlled environments with all but the most salient variables eliminated, but such sterile settings are often __________ laughter.
答案：A, D　inimical to; adverse to

They came to the conclusion that the fashion industry (ii) ____________ the piracy of new designs: design copying contributes to a process of induced obsolescence by diffusing new designs into the mainstream, causing them to lose their insider appeal. A market for new designs is thus (iii) ____________.The authors considered how it can be that the fashion industry continues to (i) ____________ when there are no laws that prevent one designer or company from copying another designer's original idea. They came to the conclusion that the fashion industry (ii) ____________ the piracy of new designs: design copying contributes to a process of induced obsolescence by diffusing new designs into the mainstream, causing them to lose their insider appeal. A market for new designs is thus (iii) ____________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, H　prosper; benefits from; perpetuated

Adapted (i)________, prairie grasses have developed roots that are primed for (ii)________: if a plant's stem is chewed down even to near ground level, it will shoot right back up again, as if defying discouragement.Adapted (i)________, prairie grasses have developed roots that are primed for (ii)________: if a plant's stem is chewed down even to near ground level, it will shoot right back up again, as if defying discouragement.

Blank 2
答案：B, E　to survive grazing; regeneration

This is partly because televised courtroom dramas, which compete with soap operas, bring to the screen some of the same elements of family (i) that appeal to soap fans. And courtroom dramas have the added (ii) that the domestic arguments they depict are for real.Audiences for daytime television soap operas have declined by 83 percent since 1991. This is partly because televised courtroom dramas, which compete with soap operas, bring to the screen some of the same elements of family (i) that appeal to soap fans. Most scientific biographies put their subjects on pedestals, but times are changing, and Coffey’s book is representative of a heightened sense of (i)_______. Weaving together the lives of the leaders of modern chemistry, Coffey shows how fights over priority, backstabbing, cronyism, and grudges shaped the history of chemistry just as much as the actual discoveries did. It is an effective (ii)_______ the (iii)_______ that science is the work of utterly selfless researchers.Most scientific biographies put their subjects on pedestals, but times are changing, and Coffey’s book is representative of a heightened sense of (i)_______. Weaving together the lives of the leaders of modern chemistry, Coffey shows how fights over priority, backstabbing, cronyism, and grudges shaped the history of chemistry just as much as the actual discoveries did. It is an effective (ii)_______ the (iii)_______ that science is the work of utterly selfless researchers.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, E, G　candor; antidote to; banal notion

But the poem is more self-contained than it appears when you read only fragments of it, and its allusions are often (ii) by their contexts or by their repeated appearances in changing contexts.The poem is not nearly as(i) as it has often been said to be. Granted, it would be (ii) to claim that the poem is self-contained or that its proliferating allusions are self-explanatory. Blank 2
Blank 3

allusion: standard unit headword
#102 Sentence Equivalence
The initial, widely shared pessimism turned out to be ______, because it ignored the many things that would be done with resources left behind.The initial, widely shared pessimism turned out to be ______, because it ignored the many things that would be done with resources left behind.
答案：D, F　unfounded; unjustified
unfounded
unfounded: standard unit headword
#103 TC SINGLE
The detours taken in the course of the essay, which could easily have been __________, instead proved to be a virtue because they epitomized the instability of a period characterized by constant experimentation and change.The detours taken in the course of the essay, which could easily have been __________, instead proved to be a virtue because they epitomized the instability of a period characterized by constant experimentation and change.
答案：A　perplexing
virtue
virtue: standard unit headword
#104 TC DOUBLE
Because self-defense was peripheral to the karate training techniques instituted throughout the Okinawan educational system in the early twentieth century, the pugilistic dimension of karate techniques was (i) __________ or even (ii) __________.Because self-defense was peripheral to the karate training techniques instituted throughout the Okinawan educational system in the early twentieth century, the pugilistic dimension of karate techniques was (i) __________ or even (ii) __________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, E　intentionally downplayed; eliminated
dimension
dimension: standard unit headword
#105 SE
Although the virtues of supplementing wild fish populations with fish from hatcheries are often_______, scientific studies have indicated that fish stocking can harm the wild, native species of the water body.Although the virtues of supplementing wild fish populations with fish from hatcheries are often_______, scientific studies have indicated that fish stocking can harm the wild, native species of the water body.
答案：B, E　extolled; acclaimed
virtue
virtue: standard unit headword
#106 Sentence Equivalence
Noise suppression in phones can play an important role in making cellphone networks more efficient, since when sounds that are ______ to the meaningful signal are transmitted, precious network bandwidth is wasted.Noise suppression in phones can play an important role in making cellphone networks more efficient, since when sounds that are ______ to the meaningful signal are transmitted, precious network bandwidth is wasted.
答案：C, F　irrelevant; extraneous
extraneous
extraneous: standard unit headword

It has been demonstrated that on Earth, no single landscape feature can be used as a quantitative indicator of past climatic conditions. The same is probably true for Mars; nevertheless, as the martian landscape is not as (i) as the terrain on Earth, with no plate tectonics, no vegetation, less water, and lower erosion rates, the landscape's(ii) the climate on Mars could be (iii) .It has been demonstrated that on Earth, no single landscape feature can be used as a quantitative indicator of past climatic conditions. The same is probably true for Mars; nevertheless, as the martian landscape is not as (i) as the terrain on Earth, with no plate tectonics, no vegetation, less water, and lower erosion rates, the landscape's(ii) the climate on Mars could be (iii) .
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, E, H　complex; connection to; more direct

landscape: standard unit headword

答案：D　variegated
notion
notion: standard unit headword
#109 SE
No sensible person would seriously advise the rock superstar to _______ her former band’s musical catalog altogether in concert, since some of those songs simply must be played.No sensible person would seriously advise the rock superstar to _______ her former band’s musical catalog altogether in concert, since some of those songs simply must be played.
答案：A, C　forswear; abjure
forswear

Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, D　obfuscate; garner an artificial

Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3

#112 Sentence Equivalence
We should be more ______ than we often are when making claims about antiquity—for example, the common statement, “the ancient Athenians invented democracy,” is simply not true when put like that.We should be more ______ than we often are when making claims about antiquity—for example, the common statement, “the ancient Athenians invented democracy,” is simply not true when put like that.
答案：C, F　precise; circumspect
circumspect
circumspect: standard unit headword
#113 TC SINGLE
The environmentalist asserted that although the trend in building regulations was theoretically geared toward energy efficiency, in terms of energy consumption, new buildings typically were becoming more __________ every year.The environmentalist asserted that although the trend in building regulations was theoretically geared toward energy efficiency, in terms of energy consumption, new buildings typically were becoming more __________ every year.
答案：C　profligate
term
term: standard unit headword
#114 TC DOUBLE
Where there has been extensive natural or human-caused disturbance, pines (i) __________ the landscape because they colonize soon after disturbance, grow rapidly, and (ii) __________ drought, cool temperatures, and nutrient-poor soils.Where there has been extensive natural or human-caused disturbance, pines (i) __________ the landscape because they colonize soon after disturbance, grow rapidly, and (ii) __________ drought, cool temperatures, and nutrient-poor soils.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, F　can achieve dominance throughout; endure

答案：C, D　dubious; questionable

答案：B, F　conspicuous; prominent

Blank 1

答案：E　a palliative to

Thus statisticians, who by virtue of their training are (ii) __________ identifying (iii) __________ interpretations of patterns in data, must increasingly bear the burden of scrutinizing data and challenging conclusions based on statistical flukes.While many tools can automatically locate patterns in large data sets, they cannot yet distinguish truly significant patterns from those that are (i) __________. Thus statisticians, who by virtue of their training are (ii) __________ identifying (iii) __________ interpretations of patterns in data, must increasingly bear the burden of scrutinizing data and challenging conclusions based on statistical flukes.

Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, E, G　merely fortuitous; adept at; spurious

答案：E　segregation

#125 SE

答案：C, F　undermine; contradict

The poem is not nearly as (i) __________ as it has often been said to be. Granted, it would be (ii) __________ to claim that the poem is self-contained or that its proliferating allusions are self-explanatory. But the poem is more self-contained than it appears when you read only fragments of it, and its allusions are often (iii) __________ by their contexts or by their repeated appearances in changing contexts.The poem is not nearly as (i) __________ as it has often been said to be. Granted, it would be (ii) __________ to claim that the poem is self-contained or that its proliferating allusions are self-explanatory. Blank 2

答案：C, F, H　difficult; absurd; explained

Blank 1

答案：C, D　dolorous; lugubrious

答案：C　inscrutable

Singers of the tropicalist movement in Brazil goaded the government by purposefully invoking stereotypical images of Brazil as a tropical paradise only to (i) ____________ them with pointed references to political and social problems. But (ii) ____________ in giving offense, the tropicalist singers also managed to annoy the Brazilian left, which shared their social concerns. They embraced an irreverent politics and (iii) ____________, epitomized by the coexistence of bossa nova and rock in the same song, that ignored certain leftist calls for folkloric authenticity.Singers of the tropicalist movement in Brazil goaded the government by purposefully invoking stereotypical images of Brazil as a tropical paradise only to (i) ____________ them with pointed references to political and social problems. But (ii) ____________ in giving offense, the tropicalist singers also managed to annoy the Brazilian left, which shared their social concerns. They embraced an irreverent politics and (iii) ____________, epitomized by the coexistence of bossa nova and rock in the same song, that ignored certain leftist calls for folkloric authenticity.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, H　subvert; ecumenical; a pastiche of styles
transitory
transitory: standard unit headword

答案：E, F　lugubrious; gloomy
lugubrious
lugubrious: standard unit headword

Therefore. a complete lack of any (i)________, human emotions in ancestral species is (ii)________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, F　antecedents of; improbable

答案：E　propitious

Singer was a prominent and beloved figure on the American literary scene for so long that it is hard to imagine that the flurry of publications marking the centennial of his birth would have anything ____________ to say about him.I.B. Singer was a prominent and beloved figure on the American literary scene for so long that it is hard to imagine that the flurry of publications marking the centennial of his birth would have anything ____________ to say about him.
答案：C, E　novel; fresh

Furthermore, even if new oil finds elsewhere have been (ii)______ than in the past, substantial quantities of oil can be profitably stripped from tar and shale.Fears that the recent sharp rise in oil prices is an indication that oil is running out appear to be (i)______. The Middle East still contains vast oil supplies. Furthermore, even if new oil finds elsewhere have been (ii)______ than in the past, substantial quantities of oil can be profitably stripped from tar and shale.

Certain molecular studies imply that, contrary to conventional scientific wisdom, neornithines (modern birds) lived alongside dinosaurs instead of having evolved after dinosaurs' extinction. It seems odd to imagine a duck paddling alongside a Spinosaurus, but the molecular evidence for the (i)_____________ of modern birds and dinosaurs is so (ii)_____________ that even paleontologists--who have typically viewed with skepticism those DNA findings that conflict with the fossil record--have begun to (iii)____________ it.Certain molecular studies imply that, contrary to conventional scientific wisdom, neornithines (modern birds) lived alongside dinosaurs instead of having evolved after dinosaurs' extinction. It seems odd to imagine a duck paddling alongside a Spinosaurus, but the molecular evidence for the (i)_____________ of modern birds and dinosaurs is so (ii)_____________ that even paleontologists--who have typically viewed with skepticism those DNA findings that conflict with the fossil record--have begun to (iii)____________ it.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, G　contemporaneity; compelling; embrace

I do not consider such worries (ii)______, though they are often based on intuitive feeling rather than on strictly logical arguments.

People love to talk about their commutes to and from work: those with an easy commute tend to (i)______, while those who hate their commute think and speak of it as a core affliction, like a chronic illness. Once you raise the subject, the testimonies pour out, and, if your ears are tuned to it, you begin overhearing commute talk everywhere. People who are normally (ii)______ may, when describing their commutes, be unexpectedly (iii)______ divulging the intimate details of their lives.People love to talk about their commutes to and from work: those with an easy commute tend to (i)______, while those who hate their commute think and speak of it as a core affliction, like a chronic illness. Once you raise the subject, the testimonies pour out, and, if your ears are tuned to it, you begin overhearing commute talk everywhere. People who are normally (ii)______ may, when describing their commutes, be unexpectedly (iii)______ divulging the intimate details of their lives.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, F, G　gloat; circumspect; candid in

#157 Sentence Equivalence
Agencies responsible for protecting natural resources too often issue permits allowing exploitation of those resources, a process that remains ______ given that agencies have become experts at masking their decisions in scientific terms.Agencies responsible for protecting natural resources too often issue permits allowing exploitation of those resources, a process that remains ______ given that agencies have become experts at masking their decisions in scientific terms.
答案：C, E　obscure; opaque

term: standard unit headword
#158 SE

答案：B, E　reconciliation; rapprochement

#159 Text Completion
One way to predict the effects of global climates change on an ecosystem is to extrapolate current trends in global change factors into the future. A (i)______ of this method is that its predictions (ii)______ actual observations, but the method also makes the questionable assumptions that the future will resemble the present.One way to predict the effects of global climates change on an ecosystem is to extrapolate current trends in global change factors into the future. A (i)______ of this method is that its predictions (ii)______ actual observations, but the method also makes the questionable assumptions that the future will resemble the present.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, E　virtue; derive from

virtue: standard unit headword
#160 Sentence Equivalence

答案：B, C　immaterial; inconsequential

答案：B, C　extrapolation; projection

While recognizing that recent reports of cyberwarfare, phone-hacking scandals, and identity thefts have tended to accent the destructive connotation of the word, Sue Halpern maintains that “hacking” is such ______ term that its meaning nearly always derives from its context.While recognizing that recent reports of cyberwarfare, phone-hacking scandals, and identity thefts have tended to accent the destructive connotation of the word, Sue Halpern maintains that “hacking” is such ______ term that its meaning nearly always derives from its context.
答案：A, B　a generic; an inclusive

term: standard unit headword
#164 SE
One object of examining past medical practices is to identify useful knowledge amid its now outmoded trappings; the alternative is to let such knowledge __________ along with the discredited theories with which it was associated.One object of examining past medical practices is to identify useful knowledge amid its now outmoded trappings; the alternative is to let such knowledge __________ along with the discredited theories with which it was associated.
答案：A, D　fade; wane

alternative: standard unit headword
#165 Text Completion
Given children’s active fantasy lives, one might think of truthfulness as (i)______ virtue in young children, but it turns out that lying is the more (ii)______ skill. A child who is going to lie must recognize the truth, intellectually conceive of an alternate reality, and be able to convincingly sell that new reality to someone else. Therefore, lying (iii)______ cognitive development and social skills in a way that honesty simply does not.Given children’s active fantasy lives, one might think of truthfulness as (i)______ virtue in young children, but it turns out that lying is the more (ii)______ skill. A child who is going to lie must recognize the truth, intellectually conceive of an alternate reality, and be able to convincingly sell that new reality to someone else. Therefore, lying (iii)______ cognitive development and social skills in a way that honesty simply does not.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, I　an acquired; advanced; demands

答案：A, D　wealth; profusion

答案：B, D　dampen; impede

Medieval cathedrals still stand as marvels of architecture, but as far as modern science is concerned, medieval physics and chemistry are simply irrelevant, at best a dead end, at worst the very ______ of what science is supposed to be.Medieval cathedrals still stand as marvels of architecture, but as far as modern science is concerned, medieval physics and chemistry are simply irrelevant, at best a dead end, at worst the very ______ of what science is supposed to be.
答案：E　antithesis

#170 SE
Our mass media are much more fascinated by bad ideas or the failure of good ones than by successes: we drown in bad news--tales of how things went wrong-and, consequently, we are lacking in any _________ discussion on how they might go right.Our mass media are much more fascinated by bad ideas or the failure of good ones than by successes: we drown in bad news--tales of how things went wrong-and, consequently, we are lacking in any _________ discussion on how they might go right.
答案：A, C　incisive; trenchant

cursory: standard unit headword
#171 Text Completion
For Plato the art of music was so (i)______ moral and political reality that any alteration to music system would necessarily require a corresponding political shift. But two and a half millennia later, when classical music is generally seen merely as a lifestyle accessory, Plato’s conception seems (ii)______. To be sure, there are still people who consider classical music to be of (iii)______ cultural importance, but few of them are able to articulate this convincingly.For Plato the art of music was so (i)______ moral and political reality that any alteration to music system would necessarily require a corresponding political shift. But two and a half millennia later, when classical music is generally seen merely as a lifestyle accessory, Plato’s conception seems (ii)______. To be sure, there are still people who consider classical music to be of (iii)______ cultural importance, but few of them are able to articulate this convincingly.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, E, H　firmly anchored in; absurd; profound
alterationarticulate
alteration: standard unit headword | articulate: standard unit headword

答案：D, F　reinforcement; bolstering

#176 Text Completion
It is possible for human to go 40 or more hours without sleep and still be able to (i)______ information acquired at the beginning of the sleepless period. Thus, when we are considering a role for sleep in human memory consolidation, we are referring to a possible role in the (ii)______ encoding of information and optimizing of recall, not a (iii)______ of sleep for recalling events of the prior day.It is possible for human to go 40 or more hours without sleep and still be able to (i)______ information acquired at the beginning of the sleepless period. Thus, when we are considering a role for sleep in human memory consolidation, we are referring to a possible role in the (ii)______ encoding of information and optimizing of recall, not a (iii)______ of sleep for recalling events of the prior day.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, G　disgorge; longer-term; requirement

#177 SE
Some renewable energy technologies are quite ____________; for example, hydro power from dams and rivers has been a source of power generation since the early 1900s, and today it supplies sixteen percent of electricity worldwide.Some renewable energy technologies are quite ____________; for example, hydro power from dams and rivers has been a source of power generation since the early 1900s, and today it supplies sixteen percent of electricity worldwide.
答案：C, D　mainstream; conventional

It may follow, then, that a lack of flexibility in certain plans may actually be a (ii)______ . Unfortunately, this (ii)______ notion recently has (iii)______ several West Coast mayors, who have in consequence eagerly pursued the implementation of ferry service in their cities.For many years, Americans have had a love affair with ferryboats. Ferries are said to relieve our frayed nerves after we’ve stewed in bumper-to-bumper traffic, and conventional wisdom also says ferries (i)______ congestion and air pollution by getting us out of our cars. Blank 2
答案：A, F　benefit; remain complacent about

答案：A, C　abused; misused

答案：C, F　burgeoning; expansion

Richard is (ii)______ and is fastidious of her appearance. So one could hardly accuse her of being either taciturn or (iii)______ .Cynics will dismiss the race between Richard and Gorman as two equally dull candidates. However, the notion that the two leaders are (i)______ does not meet them, for they are as different as can be. Richard is (ii)______ and is fastidious of her appearance. So one could hardly accuse her of being either taciturn or (iii)______ .

Blank 2
Blank 3

Most populations are affected by a combination of adverse human pressures, each of which is in itself insufficient to (i)______ a population crash. Therefore, studies of population decline that (ii)______ individual factors and thus (iii)______ potential interactions may lead to improper management of declining species.Human-caused disturbances, such as habitat destruction and the introduction of nonnative species, are among the leading causes of plant and animal population declines. Most populations are affected by a combination of adverse human pressures, each of which is in itself insufficient to (i)______ a population crash. Blank 2

The process of copying manuscripts, the only way in which the fragile products of centuries of accumulating knowledge could be preserved, (iii)______ , a situation that did not change for two and a half centuries, until the time of Charlemagne. In the intervening period, much of Classical literature was lost to use forever.For a time in the early Middle Ages, Latin culture came close to (i)______ : the witness to that is the (ii)______ of manuscript copies of texts datable to the period. The process of copying manuscripts, the only way in which the fragile products of centuries of accumulating knowledge could be preserved, (iii)______ , a situation that did not change for two and a half centuries, until the time of Charlemagne. In the intervening period, much of Classical literature was lost to use forever.

Blank 3
答案：C, D, I　vanishing; paucity; virtually came to a standstill

The notion that childhood vaccines are driving autism rates has been (ii)______ by multiple epidemiological studies. One memory can seem (ii)______ whereas another must be coaxed out of our brain little by little. Part of what currently makes it so (i)______ to arrive at a scientific understanding of the living world is that while technological advances have produced a cascade of data—from detailed genome sequence to the sophisticated satellite imagery that documents the planet’s ecosystems—our ability to (ii)______ these data still lags far behind their (iii)______.Part of what currently makes it so (i)______ to arrive at a scientific understanding of the living world is that while technological advances have produced a cascade of data—from detailed genome sequence to the sophisticated satellite imagery that documents the planet’s ecosystems—our ability to (ii)______ these data still lags far behind their (iii)______.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, E, G　challenging; apprehend; acquisition

Not only was Pushkin’s personal correspondence intercepted and his movements (iii)______, but Tsar Nicholas I’s decision to oversee Pushkin’s career obliged Pushkin to submit all his manuscripts for inspection.Even the reader acquainted with the outlines of Pushkin’s biography will be (i)______ the (ii)______ so vividly conveyed in Binyon’s biography. Blank 1

答案：C, D, H　struck by; suffocating lack of creative freedom; monitored

答案：D　small

But it’s (i)______ to (ii)______ all 2,600 described species of mosquito when it’s just 80 or so—3 percent—that drink human blood. Among those 2,520 relatively (iii)______ kinds of mosquitoes, there’s even one we’d like to see in greater numbers: Taxorhynchites, the mosquito that eats other mosquitoes.There is no sense trying to rehabilitate the reputation of the mosquito; nobody loves such a creature. It is heavy on dialogue (and light on embellishment), features very short sections (of often only a few sentence) in rapid succession, and in general favors an unadorned directness. Not surprisingly, this style works better in short-story form than in novels, and the story collection I Wish Someone Were Waiting for me Somewhere (ii)______ her attempt to (iii)______ it in a novel, as in, for example, Someone I Loved.Anna Gavalda’s fiction is appealing largely because of her writing styles, which is certainly (i)______. Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, F, H　distinctive; impresses considerably more than; sustain

Yet the traditional commitment to (ii)______ was not simply (iii)______ : this new genre was expected to capture the essence of historical truth even as it allowed space for the writer’s imagination.

答案：B, D, G　factuality; veracity; jettisoned

There is no evidence that other species have ever exercised such (i)______ effects. That lack of evidence could merely be because the other species have been studied less thoroughly. However, the size and density of the historic sea otter populations in many Pacific coastal regions (ii)______ those of other otter species elsewhere, so perhaps the sea otter is indeed (iii)______ in its effects on habitat.At least one otter species, the sea otter, has a large, often dominating, effect on the structure of its own habitat. There is no evidence that other species have ever exercised such (i)______ effects. That lack of evidence could merely be because the other species have been studied less thoroughly. However, the size and density of the historic sea otter populations in many Pacific coastal regions (ii)______ those of other otter species elsewhere, so perhaps the sea otter is indeed (iii)______ in its effects on habitat.

Blank 2

答案：C, D, G　significant; vastly exceed; unique

Blank 2

答案：C, F, H　dogmatic; qualify; plentiful

#311 Text Completion
There are no ______ criteria of excellence in art: works that once were ignored or even reviled now fetch millions of dollars at auction, while those that were most highly praised in their day now languish in storage.There are no ______ criteria of excellence in art: works that once were ignored or even reviled now fetch millions of dollars at auction, while those that were most highly praised in their day now languish in storage.
答案：D　immutable

#312 Text Completion
Although the rich history of filmed versions of Shakespeare’s plays reaches back to the early twentieth century, filmmakers still appear to (i)______. They typically seek to (ii)______ their work by claiming that film techniques can improve upon Shakespeare’s text and on the conditions of theatrical performance, and yet such claims, asserted too strenuously, can seem defensive.Although the rich history of filmed versions of Shakespeare’s plays reaches back to the early twentieth century, filmmakers still appear to (i)______. They typically seek to (ii)______ their work by claiming that film techniques can improve upon Shakespeare’s text and on the conditions of theatrical performance, and yet such claims, asserted too strenuously, can seem defensive.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, E　doubt their own legitimacy; validate

Much scholarship on the early Tana Tradition of coastal East Africa has been characterized by faith in the (i)______ of that tradition, but detailed analysis of ceramics suggests that there were (ii)______ the decorative motifs on early Tana Tradition jars, making it difficult to view the various Tana communities as a monolithic whole.Much scholarship on the early Tana Tradition of coastal East Africa has been characterized by faith in the (i)______ of that tradition, but detailed analysis of ceramics suggests that there were (ii)______ the decorative motifs on early Tana Tradition jars, making it difficult to view the various Tana communities as a monolithic whole.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, D　homogeneity; regional differences in

#314 Text Completion
The art of letter writing is like the art of acting in that it is the impression of (i)______ which usually makes a performance convincing. Great letter writers, like great actors, have a gift for (ii)______ , for the here and now, the depth of expression depending on its closeness to actual processes of thought. By these criteria Ellen Terry was a remarkable letter writer indeed: her letters invariably have an air of (iii)______ .The art of letter writing is like the art of acting in that it is the impression of (i)______ which usually makes a performance convincing. Great letter writers, like great actors, have a gift for (ii)______ , for the here and now, the depth of expression depending on its closeness to actual processes of thought. By these criteria Ellen Terry was a remarkable letter writer indeed: her letters invariably have an air of (iii)______ .
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, F, H　spontaneity; immediacy; improvisation

He is among the most uninhibited comic performers around, but here he buttons his lip and stares straight ahead. Perhaps without quite knowing it, the audience waits for a wink, a hint that some of the (ii)______ spirit that animated his previous movies might be lurking inside the (iii)______ manner he presents in this film.His new role gives the normally clownish actor a chance to impress audiences with his (i)______ . He is among the most uninhibited comic performers around, but here he buttons his lip and stares straight ahead. Perhaps without quite knowing it, the audience waits for a wink, a hint that some of the (ii)______ spirit that animated his previous movies might be lurking inside the (iii)______ manner he presents in this film.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, H　forbearance; madcap; reserved

It reveals as (i)______ and (ii)______ some values that have often been regarded as (iii)______, while uncovering other values that do indeed seem to be universal.

Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, E, H　transient; relative; nonnegotiable

#320 Text Completion
One sometimes hears that Marco Polo introduced pasta to the western world, having encountered it in China. This durable myth, which (i)______ that nothing should have been known of pasta in Italy until 1295, when Marco Polo returned from the Far East, can easily be (ii)______ by pointing out that there are Italian references to pasta that (iii)______.One sometimes hears that Marco Polo introduced pasta to the western world, having encountered it in China. This durable myth, which (i)______ that nothing should have been known of pasta in Italy until 1295, when Marco Polo returned from the Far East, can easily be (ii)______ by pointing out that there are Italian references to pasta that (iii)______.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, E, I　requires; debunked; predated that event

virtue: standard unit headword
#321 Text Completion
Computers make it spectacularly easy to search for particular pieces of information in downloaded texts. And doing research in this strategic, targeted manner can feel (i)______. Instead of (ii)______ the organizing logic of the book you are reading, you can approach the book with your own questions and (iii)______. You, not the author, are the master.Computers make it spectacularly easy to search for particular pieces of information in downloaded texts. And doing research in this strategic, targeted manner can feel (i)______. Instead of (ii)______ the organizing logic of the book you are reading, you can approach the book with your own questions and (iii)______. You, not the author, are the master.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, E, H　empowering; surrendering to; glean precisely what you want from it
